prerequisites: A Mailchimp account with access to the API Keys section of account settings.

Your server prefix (data center), which is the leading segment of your Mailchimp account URL (for example, the us19 in https://us19.admin.mailchimp.com/). This maps to the connector's data_center config field.
For OAuth: a registered app from Mailchimp's Registered Apps page, which provides a client_id and client_secret, plus a redirect URI that Mailchimp redirects users to after they consent.
Optionally, a start_date to bound the earliest records replicated, and num_workers to control parallelism.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Generate a key in the API Keys section of your Mailchimp account (Create A Key), then supply it as credentials.apikey. The data center is set via the data_center config field. Requests authenticate with HTTP basic auth using any username and the key as the password.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Register an app on Mailchimp's Registered Apps page to obtain credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret. After the user authorizes via https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/authorize, exchange the returned code at https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/token for credentials.access_token. The server prefix (data center) is retrieved from the metadata endpoint https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/metadata.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Click your profile icon and choose Profile, open the Extras drop-down and choose API keys, then click Create A Key and give it a descriptive name. Copy the generated key immediately and store it securely, as you will only be able to see the full key right after you generate it. Provide it as credentials.apikey.

setupSteps.title: Determine your server prefix (data center)
setupSteps.body: Log into Mailchimp and look at the browser URL, for example https://us19.admin.mailchimp.com/. The leading segment (us19) is your server prefix. Set this as the data_center config field.

setupSteps.title: Or set up OAuth 2.0
setupSteps.body: Instead of an API key, register an app on Mailchimp's Registered Apps page to get client_id and client_secret. Direct the user to https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/authorize, exchange the returned authorization code at https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/token for an access token, then call https://login.mailchimp.com/oauth2/metadata to get the server prefix. Provide credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.access_token.

setupSteps.title: Configure replication options
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to limit how far back records are pulled, and num_workers to tune the number of parallel workers used during sync.
